name: Sendero El Saltillo from Canillas del Aceituno
name_original: Sendero El Saltillo desde Canillas del Aceituno
region_intro: Hiking around Canillas De Aceituno offers access to the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, characterized by mountainous terrain with white and grey marble formations, pine forests, and Mediterranean vegetation. The village, situated at an altitude of around 645 meters, provides views across the Axarquía region and the Mediterranean Sea. The area features rivers and streams that have carved dramatic gorges and waterfalls. Best hiking trails around Canillas De Aceituno The most popular hiking route is Sendero El Saltillo from Canillas del Aceituno, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route is known for its cliff-clinging walkways and a significant suspension bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Maroma from Canillas del Aceituno (SL-A 142), a difficult 11.1 miles (17.8 km) path. This trail leads to the highest peak in Málaga province, offering extensive views. Local hikers also love the Waterfalls of the Bermuza River through the Caves of La Fájara – circular from Canillas del Aceituno, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through gorges and past waterfalls, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Canillas De Aceituno is defined by mountainous terrain, dramatic gorges, and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Canillas De Aceituno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 2800 hikers have used komoot to explore Canillas De Aceituno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Canillas de Aceituno?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes around Canillas de Aceituno, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 8 easy, 15 moderate, and 7 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: The best times to hike in Canillas de Aceituno are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations like La Maroma.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: Yes, there are 8 easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed walk. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the village itself offers gentle strolls, and some sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2908273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia Trail from Canillas de Aceituno&lt;/a&gt; provide accessible paths with scenic views.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, which surrounds Canillas de Aceituno. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check local regulations for specific trails.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Hiking in Canillas de Aceituno offers diverse natural features. You can explore dramatic gorges carved by rivers like the Almanchares, discover waterfalls, and encounter Mediterranean vegetation. Notable landmarks include the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6391884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Ferrata After the Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995959146/waterfalls-of-the-bermuza-river-through-the-caves-of-la-fajara-circular-from-canillas-del-aceituno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls of the Bermuza River through the Caves of La Fájara – circular from Canillas del Aceituno&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9-mile (11.2 km) loop that takes you through gorges and past waterfalls, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: Canillas de Aceituno offers various parking options within the village. For popular trails like El Saltillo, there are designated parking areas near the trailhead or within the village itself. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trails?
A: Public transport to Canillas de Aceituno is limited. While there are bus services connecting the village to larger towns in the Axarquía region, these may not always align directly with trailhead access or hiking schedules. It's often more convenient to reach the area by car.

Q: What makes the hike to La Maroma challenging?
A: The hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995954037/la-maroma-from-canillas-del-aceituno-sl-a-142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma from Canillas del Aceituno (SL-A 142)&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to its significant length of 11.1 miles (17.8 km) and substantial elevation gain of over 1,350 meters. It leads to the highest peak in Málaga province, requiring good physical fitness and preparation for a long ascent.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: The trails in Canillas de Aceituno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views, the unique experience of the cliff-clinging walkways and suspension bridge on the El Saltillo route, and the diverse natural beauty of the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for day hiking on most established trails within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. However, for certain activities like canyoning or accessing very sensitive areas, it's always best to check with the park authorities for any specific requirements or restrictions.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Canillas de Aceituno?
A: Yes, Canillas de Aceituno is a charming white village with amenities for visitors. You can find local restaurants and cafes offering traditional Andalusian cuisine. There are also various accommodation options, including guesthouses and rural lodgings, making it a convenient base for exploring the surrounding hiking trails.
tips: For the break on site, you should have a jacket with you when you are not on the road in midsummer, because it is pretty cool there.
